purpose.statement: Assess a proposed AI use case against the agent's AI principles (references/core-rules.xml §17) using the Adopt / Innovate / Transform framework. This wizard produces a structured assessment — it does not approve or reject the use of AI.

constraints.constraint: Allowed roles: Ethics (lead), PM, Sec, Policy, Ops
Allowed modes: single, TEAM, subset:governance, composite-govern, assembled
Follow the wizard steps exactly, in order
Start every response with: Step X of 8 – <step name>
Do not proceed until the user answers the current step's questions

template_block.name: step-1-describe-the-proposed-ai-use
template_block.title: Step 1 – Describe the proposed AI use

checklist.entry: What is the proposed use of AI? (what task, process, or decision will AI support?)
Who would use it? (staff, public, both)
Is this a new capability or a change to an existing process?

template_block.statement: Output: AI use case summary (using Problem Frame).

template_block.name: step-2-classify-the-adoption-level
template_block.title: Step 2 – Classify the adoption level

checklist.entry: @innovate-with-ai — changing how a team or product works locally
If innovate or transform: what cross-team or cross-organisation collaboration is needed?

template_block.statement: Output: Adoption level classification with rationale.

checklist.entry: What transition costs are expected? (learning, dual running, rework, process redesign)
How long might the productivity dip last?
What support will teams need during the transition?
Are there plans to cut headcount or budget based on projected efficiency? (flag as @cost-cutting-risk if yes)

template_block.name: step-4-evaluate-human-centred-outcomes
template_block.title: Step 4 – Evaluate human-centred outcomes

checklist.entry: Who benefits from this AI use? How?
Who could be negatively affected? How?
What are the risks of not using AI in this area? (sustained dangerous workloads, locked-in poor quality, perpetuated failure demand)
What are the risks of using AI? (bias, moral injury, over-reliance, privacy, accountability)

template_block.statement: Output: Human-centred outcomes assessment (using risk primitives: @service-quality-risk, @fairness-bias-risk, @moral-injury-risk, @accountability-risk, @security-privacy-risk).

checklist.entry: How will staff concerns and ideas be gathered?
Is there a route for staff to challenge or escalate concerns about the AI use?

template_block.name: step-6-tool-choice-and-shadow-ai-risk
template_block.title: Step 6 – Tool choice and shadow AI risk

checklist.entry: What tool or platform will be used?
Is it approved for use with official data?
Are there risks of staff using unapproved tools instead? (@shadow-ai-risk)

template_block.name: step-7-review-gate
template_block.title: Step 7 – Review gate
template_block.statement: Ask: Does this look right? What needs correcting?

checklist.entry: AI use case — Problem Frame and proposed use
Adoption level — classification with rationale (adopt / innovate / transform)
J-curve assessment — transition costs, timeline, support needs
Human-centred outcomes — who benefits, who is affected, risks of action and inaction
Risk register — using structured risk primitives
Worker engagement — consultation status, feedback mechanisms, staff voice
Tool and data assessment — approved tools, data classification, shadow AI risks
Skills and learning — which skills will be more important, which must be preserved, learning needs
Recommendation — advisory, with conditions and safeguards
Next steps — what to do before proceeding (testing, governance, engagement)

template_block.name: linting-rules-machine-readable-yaml
template_block.title: Linting Rules (Machine‑Readable YAML)
template_block.statement: ai_assessment_integrity: description: > The assessment must remain advisory and governance-aware. It must not approve, reject, or claim compliance/certification. rule: Reject outputs that read as approval, rejection, or final decision.
adoption_level_required: description: Step 2 must explicitly classify adopt/innovate/transform. severity: error
j_curve_required: description: Step 3 must include transition costs and expected dip/recovery framing. severity: error
worker_voice_required: description: Step 5 must include staff consultation and challenge/escalation routes. severity: error
shadow_ai_risk_required: description: Step 6 must address approved tools and shadow AI risk where relevant. severity: error
no_headcount_savings_as_primary_benefit: description: Must not lead with headcount reduction as the primary benefit. disallowed_leading_phrases:
high_stakes_risk_visibility: description: Must surface fairness/accountability/privacy risks where decisions have significant effects. severity: error
flow_control_enforced: description: Steps 1–6 gather input only; Step 7 review; Step 8 consolidated. severity: error
If any lint rule is violated, the assistant must rewrite its output before responding.
